name: Soto de Viñuelas Loop
name_original: Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares – circular desde Tres Cantos por la Dehesa Boyal – gravel
description: Cycle a moderate 25.7-mile gravel route through the scenic Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares and Dehesa Boyal from Tres Canto
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tres Cantos are situated just north of Madrid, within a privileged natural setting. The region is characterized by its location within the expansive Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, and borders the Montes del Pardo and Viñuelas. This area features varied landscapes, from gentle undulations to more challenging ascents towards the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills, with streams like Tejada, Valdecarrizo, and Bodonal weaving through the terrain. An 11-mile (18 km) "Anillo Verde" (Green Ring) also encircles the city, providing urban green spaces and natural corridors for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Tres Cantos The most popular touring cycling route is Soto de Viñuelas Loop, a 25.7 miles (41 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural environment of Soto de Viñuelas, known for its Mediterranean biodiversity. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bridges of Grajal and the Marmot - circular from Tres Cantos - gravel, a difficult 21.3 miles (34 km) path. This route features historic bridges, including Puente de la Marmota, and offers gravel sections. Local touring cyclists also love the Descent to Tres Cantos – Viñuelas Stream Bridge loop from Tres Cantos, a 21.3 miles (34 km) trail leading through varied terrain alongside the Viñuelas Stream, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Tres Cantos is defined by the diverse landscapes of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, the adjacent Montes del Pardo and Viñuelas, and the urban "Anillo Verde". The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding ascents. The routes in Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tres Cantos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Tres Cantos?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Tres Cantos, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy loops to more challenging ascents towards the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962366281/soto-de-vinuelas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto de Viñuelas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41 km trail that explores the natural environment of Soto de Viñuelas, suitable for various fitness levels. There are 6 easy routes in total, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos?
A: Touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos traverse diverse landscapes. You'll ride through the expansive Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, and alongside the Montes del Pardo and Viñuelas. Expect varied terrain from gentle undulations to more challenging climbs, with streams like Tejada, Valdecarrizo, and Bodonal weaving through the area. The 18 km "Anillo Verde" (Green Ring) also provides urban green spaces and natural corridors.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Tres Cantos has 27 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959736578/bridges-of-grajal-and-the-marmot-circular-from-tres-cantos-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges of Grajal and the Marmot - circular from Tres Cantos - gravel&lt;/a&gt;, a 34 km path featuring significant elevation changes and gravel sections. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10442370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelaganar Viewpoint – Colmenar Viejo Train Station loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers substantial climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6947355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Tres Cantos – Viñuelas Stream Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 34 km circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14931422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Cantos Train Station – Arched Stone Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers a convenient circular option.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can discover several interesting attractions. Look out for historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Stream Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. For stunning views, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any gravel routes for touring cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos caters to gravel cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959736578/bridges-of-grajal-and-the-marmot-circular-from-tres-cantos-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges of Grajal and the Marmot - circular from Tres Cantos - gravel&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated gravel route that takes you through varied terrain and past historic bridges.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Tres Cantos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the natural environment of Soto de Viñuelas to the challenging ascents towards the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Can I reach the touring cycling routes by public transport?
A: Tres Cantos is well-connected, and some routes are accessible via public transport. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14931422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Cantos Train Station – Arched Stone Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; starts directly from the Tres Cantos Train Station, making it convenient for those arriving by rail.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tres Cantos?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate and proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Tres Cantos. Temperatures are mild, and the natural landscapes, including the holm oaks and cork oaks, are vibrant. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos is a modern city with various parking options. Many routes start near urban areas or parks where public parking is available. For routes beginning from the city center or train station, you'll typically find designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking.
tips: A dehesa boyal is what was formerly called a meadow intended for grazing oxen (hence boyal) or cattle in general. Currently, the use of this Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes is merely recreational, with a spa and a sports center on the land.

One of the places to highlight if you do the route through the soto de Viñuelas wall.
More at https://bigjoe.es/ruta-mtb-el-muro-de-soto-de-vinuelas/
This track is a traditional livestock route, still in use, so the surface is in a variable state in various sections. It is recommended to go down the central path.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain. You'll find primarily paved surfaces, making it accessible, but also unpaved segments that can be more challenging. Especially within the Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes section, expect a mix of paths and trails with continuous ups and downs. Be aware that you might encounter loose stones or ruts, particularly after rainfall.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: Most touring cyclists can expect to complete this route in approximately 3 hours and 8 minutes, depending on their pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: Is this gravel cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate' and requiring good fitness, the route is also considered suitable for all skill levels. The mix of paved and unpaved sections, along with some continuous ups and downs, means beginners should be prepared for some challenges, especially if conditions are wet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Tres Cantos?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Tres Cantos, you can look for public parking options within the town. Specific parking areas near the Dehesa Boyal are generally available, but it's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the scenic Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares and the Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes, known for its holm oak forests. You'll also pass the Viñuelas Stream Bridge and experience a notable Descent to Tres Cantos. The natural park setting ensures picturesque views throughout.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta Verde 6.6 Carril Bici de Colmenar, Ruta Verde 7.8 Tres Cantos - El Pardo, GR 124 MTB (Senda Real), and the Madrid Way of Saint James, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares – circular from Tres Cantos por la Dehesa Boyal – gravel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares generally allows dogs, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, especially within the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, including the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas. However, as it's a protected natural area, it's crucial to respect all environmental regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and some unpaved sections might become muddy after heavy winter rains. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the route in Tres Cantos?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Tres Cantos, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs within the town where you can refuel before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes
